Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Nothing changed (except the timer). The timer is now at 3 pixels. I'm running out of time!
Let me think about what I haven't tried. I've tried clicking on:
- Light green container (cut and grow)
- Yellow container (cut and grow)
- Light green U-shapes (cut and grow)
- Yellow U-shapes (cut and grow)
- Red crosses (no effect)
- G separator bars (no effect)
- Gray containers (no effect)
- Dark gray inner containers (no effect)
- Timer bar (decreases timer)
- Black centers of red crosses (no effect)
What I haven't tried:
- Clicking on the charcoal (c) inner containers
- Clicking on empty black spaces
- Clicking on the G bars of the yellow container
Let me try clicking on the charcoal inner container at (20, 39), which is inside the left dark gray container.
Actually, looking at the ascii:
Row 19: g ccccc G ccccc g
Row 20: c NNN c c NNN c
The charcoal pixels at row 20 are at cols 37, 41, 43, 47.
Let me try clicking on (20, 37), which is a charcoal pixel inside the left dark gray container.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the charcoal pixel at (20, 37) inside the left dark gray container
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 20, 'col': 37}])
print(f"Result: board_changed={result.get('board_changed')}, done={result.get('done')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'c':
        print(f"Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, px={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
